intitule: Reading Comprehension: Socialising with Friends and Neighbours

enonce.texte:
Read the story below and answer the questions that follow.

"Maya and her neighbour, Mrs. Ekwalla, have been friends for five years. They have always helped each other. Last week, Mrs. Ekwalla's daughter was sick, so Maya took her to the hospital. Maya has also invited Mrs. Ekwalla to many family gatherings. Mrs. Ekwalla has never forgotten Maya's kindness. Recently, they have started a small community garden together. They have planted tomatoes and peppers. Maya says, 'I have learned so much from Mrs. Ekwalla about gardening.' Their friendship has grown stronger over the years."
images: An illustration of two women, one younger and one older, working together in a small garden with tomato plants and pepper plants, smiling and talking.

questions.texte: Identify how long Maya and Mrs. Ekwalla have been friends.
questions.reponse: They have been friends for five years.

questions.texte: Explain what Maya did when Mrs. Ekwalla's daughter was sick.
questions.reponse: Maya took Mrs. Ekwalla's daughter to the hospital.

questions.texte: Justify why Mrs. Ekwalla has never forgotten Maya's kindness.
questions.reponse: Mrs. Ekwalla has never forgotten Maya's kindness because Maya has always helped her, including taking her daughter to the hospital and inviting her to family gatherings.

questions.texte: Analyse the relationship between Maya and Mrs. Ekwalla based on the text.
questions.reponse: The relationship between Maya and Mrs. Ekwalla is one of mutual respect, trust, and support. They have been friends for five years and have consistently helped each other. Their friendship has grown stronger over time, as shown by their collaboration on the community garden project.

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences using the present perfect tense of the verbs in brackets.

questions.texte: My neighbour __________ (live) in this house since 2010.
questions.reponse: My neighbour has lived in this house since 2010.

questions.texte: We __________ (not/see) our friends for two weeks.
questions.reponse: We have not seen our friends for two weeks.
Have you ever visited your neighbour's house?
She has already eaten dinner with her family.

questions.texte: They __________ (know) each other since primary school.
questions.reponse: They have known each other since primary school.
I have never met my neighbour's cousin before.

enonce.texte:
Rearrange the following sentences to form a coherent narrative paragraph about socialising with neighbours. Write the correct order (letters only).

A. Since then, we have become good friends.
B. They have lived next door for three years.
C. I first met my neighbours when they moved in.
D. We have shared many meals and celebrations together.
E. I have also helped them with their garden.

questions.texte: What is the correct order of the sentences to form a logical narrative paragraph?
questions.reponse: The correct order is: C, B, D, E, A.

sousQuestions.texte: Justify why you placed sentence 'A' at the end.
sousQuestions.reponse: I placed sentence 'A' ('Since then, we have become good friends') at the end because it serves as a concluding statement that reflects on the overall relationship, which is typical for a narrative paragraph.

enonce.texte:
Write a short narrative paragraph (about 80-100 words) about a positive experience you have had with a friend or neighbour.

Your paragraph should include:

  • A clear introduction of the person.
  • A description of what happened.
  • A conclusion about the experience.

Use at least three sentences in the present perfect tense.

questions.texte: Write your narrative paragraph about socialising with a friend or neighbour.
questions.reponse: My neighbour, Mrs. Abena, has been my friend for two years. We have always greeted each other warmly. Last month, my mother was sick, and Mrs. Abena came to help us. She has cooked many meals for our family. I have also helped her with her shopping. Her kindness has made our neighbourhood feel like a real community. I have learned that being a good neighbour means helping others.

enonce.texte:
Complete the following dialogue between two neighbours who have just met. Use the present perfect tense where appropriate.

Neighbour A: Hello! I don't think we have met before. I'm David.
Neighbour B: Hi David! I'm Sarah. I __________ (live) here for only two months.
Neighbour A: Oh, welcome! How __________ you __________ (like) the neighbourhood so far?
Neighbour B: I __________ (love) it! Everyone __________ (be) very friendly.
Neighbour A: That's great to hear. I __________ (know) many of our neighbours for years.
Neighbour B: That's wonderful. I __________ (not/meet) everyone yet.
Neighbour A: Don't worry. We __________ (have) a few community events recently. You should come to the next one!

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the correct present perfect forms of the verbs in brackets.
questions.reponse:
Neighbour A: Hello! I don't think we have met before. I'm David.
Neighbour B: Hi David! I'm Sarah. I have lived here for only two months.
Neighbour A: Oh, welcome! How have you liked the neighbourhood so far?
Neighbour B: I have loved it! Everyone has been very friendly.
Neighbour A: That's great to hear. I have known many of our neighbours for years.
Neighbour B: That's wonderful. I have not met everyone yet.
Neighbour A: Don't worry. We have had a few community events recently. You should come to the next one!

questions.texte: Identify the present perfect sentences in the completed dialogue.
questions.reponse:
1. I have lived here for only two months.
2. How have you liked the neighbourhood so far?
3. I have loved it!
4. Everyone has been very friendly.
5. I have known many of our neighbours for years.
6. I have not met everyone yet.
7. We have had a few community events recently.

enonce.texte:
Your neighbour, Mr. Ngu, has been playing loud music every night, and it has been disturbing your family. You have decided to talk to him about it. Write a short dialogue (at least 10 exchanges) where you politely discuss the problem and find a solution together.

Use the present perfect tense at least three times in your dialogue.

questions.texte: Write a dialogue between you and your neighbour about the loud music problem.
questions.reponse:
Me: Good evening, Mr. Ngu. I hope I'm not disturbing you.
Mr. Ngu: Not at all! How are you?
Me: I'm fine, thank you. I wanted to talk to you about something. Your music has been very loud every night.
Mr. Ngu: Oh, I'm sorry. I didn't realize it was disturbing you.
Me: It has kept my family awake for the past few nights.
Mr. Ngu: I understand. I have been enjoying my new speakers.
Me: I appreciate that. Could you please lower the volume after 9 PM?
Mr. Ngu: Of course! I haven't thought about it before. I will keep it down.
Me: Thank you so much! I have always appreciated having you as a neighbour.
Mr. Ngu: And I appreciate you telling me. Let's be good neighbours!
